Roger Gale MP The House of Commons London SW1A 0AA ​ ​ ​ Hello and welcome. Here you can find many of my recent press releases and newspaper articles, together with more personal details including my biography, details of groups I support and associated photographs. I have represented North Thanet since 1983 and I have always regarded it as my first priority to promote the interests of this constituency. I should like to emphasise that although I am proud to sit in the House of Commons as a supporter of the Conservative Party, I try to represent all my constituents, regardless of party politics. Please do not hesitate to get in touch with me if you need my help with a problem or have a query you would like to discuss - at any time. Just write to me at the address above (if the House is not sitting - at weekends and during recess periods - then my mail is forwarded to Kent within hours). Alternatively, you may wish to come along and see me at one of my regular constituency Advice Surgeries. In that event please contact me via the link at the top of this page. My wife, Suzy, who is my Parliamentary Office Director and my staff will be delighted to give you full details of my surgeries and to arrange an appointment for us to meet in either Herne Bay or in the Margate area. ​ ​ ​ ​ Yours sincerely, ​ With all good wishes from Suzy and myself. ​ ​ ​ ​ ​ (Promoted by Roger Gale MP of The Old Forge, 215A Canterbury Road, Birchington CT7 9AH) Latest Updates & News 22nd April 2026 World Horse Champion 18th April 2026 Save Our Fields 17th April2025 Capita Pensions Connect with Roger 01843 848588 GALERJ@PARLIAMENT.UK Read more on GDPR Law Useful Links © 2025 SLM bottom of page

Press Releases 2026 Roger regularly issues press releases about current topics. We have included a selection of them here which we hope will be of interest to you. 18th April 2026 Great turnout for the Save Our Fields “Walk and Talk,” with a John Deere tractor from Thanet farmers Pippa and James Southorn, and a strong case from Pippa for growing our own crops. Then on to Westgate Town Hall for quiz prizes, well done to Kerry Boyd (1st) and Vanessa Crow Brown (2nd), before heading to Dog Acre, Birchington, to judge the village scarecrow competition highlighting the threat to local farmland. The “Traditional Scarecrow” by Birchington in Bloom was a standout. Finally, a quick visit to Pegwell Bay to catch up with talented young filmmaker Connor Carruthers, supporting the Save Minster Marshes campaign against the proposed converter station. If we want to protect our environment, we must stand up and fight for it. 17th April 2026 Yesterday morning I took the opportunity at Business Questions to raise the issue of the failure of the company responsible, Capita, to pay, to retired civil servants, their pensions in a timely and efficient manner. This failure is causing real personal and financial distress to loyal servants of this Country and has to be resolved as a matter of urgency. ￼ The Leader of the House, as always courteous, has agreed to pursue this matter with the Cabinet Secretary. ​ ​ Watch here​ 16th April 2026 The Prime Minister appears to have developed a habit of deflecting difficult questions by claiming that matters “didn’t cross his desk” an approach that raises serious doubts about how decisions are being made at the highest level of government. It is time for him to stop shifting responsibility onto others, accept accountability, and do the honourable thing by resigning. 16th April 2026 On Saturday 18th April WESTGATE & GARLINGE action group will be taking part in the “National Day of Action for nature - to save green space from development”. Their Day of Action event starts at 10am at Dent de Lion Gatehouse with a Walk-'n'-Talk across the farmland footpath ending with a Farmer talk with a tractor at Minster Road (approximately 10.30am). There will also have an Environmental Information Hub at Westgate Town Council Offices will be open between 10-12noon. This is one of the many events happening across Thanet with Birchington having a Scarecrow Competition ( Thanet Scarecrows ) and Save Minster Marshes will be having an event by The Viking Ship in Cliffsend. ​ ​ Watch here​ 16th April 2026 A huge privilege to meet Elliott with his Mother and younger sister at a military families gathering in the House yesterday morning at the start of the month of the Military Child organised by the Royal British Legion. Elliott’s father has served for much of his twenty five years in the army overseas and Elliott’s moving poem reflects his experience as a small boy growing up with a frequently absent dad. Dad has just retired from the armed forces to join Lancashire police force and so at last will at least be based at home. Do we need to be reminded of the sacrifices made not just by our serving men and women but also by their families? Yes. I think that we do. 14th April 2026 Before the Easter recess the Health Secretary, Mr Streeting, used a live interview on Sky television to take a swipe at the maternity unit at the QEQM hospital i

pages.text_excerpt: --> Roger Gale | MP | Thanet | Herne Bay | Current Issues top of page Sir Roger Gale Member of Parliament for Herne Bay and Sandwich (including West Thanet) Home Roger's Views Press Releases Current Issues Westminster Offices & Memberships Archive Archive 2025 Archive 2024 Archive 2023 Archive 2022 Archive 2021 Archive 2020 Archive 2019 Archive 2018 Archive 2017 Archive 2016 Archive 2015 Archive 2014 Archive 2013 Archive 2012 Archive 2011 Archive 2010 Archive 2009 Archive 2008 Archive 2007 Gallery Contact Us More Use tab to navigate through the menu items. Current Issues ​ ​ ​ A number of my constituents have subscribed to e-campaigning websites which produce regular automated campaign emails to MPs. I always seek to provide replies to personal communications, but a number of my constituents suggested that, rather than sending individual and formal responses to e-lobbying and for the purposes of speed, I should make my own observations available electronically - a suggestion I have decided to adopt. As such you can find my responses to many of these campaigns, here. ABORTION While I have myself consistently voted against any relaxation of the abortion laws during my forty years in the House of Commons I am not at present, as a Deputy Speaker, able to speak or vote upon legislation in respect of this matter. I have however taken the opportunity to make my own views privately known to Ministers. ​ Please click PDF link for Adobe version ANIMAL WELFARE The Conservative Animal Welfare Foundation, of which I am a Patron, will be making a comprehensive submission in respect of these issues in good time for consideration for the Party's next General Election manifesto. I am currently acting as a Deputy Speaker in the House and I am therefore not permitted to take part in debates in the House. You may be sure that many of my colleagues will be taking an interest in these matters. ​ Please click PDF link for Adobe version BREXIT STATEMENT ​ ​ Please click PDF to read BULLY DOGS I was heavily involved in discussions surrounding the original Dangerous Dogs legislation having worked with lawyers to seek protection for Dempsey and other `Pit Bull Type` terriers. The issue of XL Bully dogs is complex. First, there is no clear definition of what constitutes an `XL Bully Dog` which is not a breed in its own right. Second there is the adage that `there are no bad dogs – only bad owners` which as a generality is true. The sad fact is that `XL` dogs are too frequently acquired by young men who do not know how to handle and /or mistreat them and that can lead to animals that are out of control and potentially vicious. The government clearly has a duty to try to prevent further horrific attacks and deaths of the kind that have been witnessed recently. There is no easy solution but I have come to the conclusion that, while not in favour of yet more bureaucracy, the creation of a canine register might be a better long-term answer than the proposal to `extend the Dangerous Dogs Act` (itself a deeply flawed piece of legislation) to embrace as yet undefined non-breeds that is currently under consideration. We need a considered response not a knee-jerk reaction to the problem and I have written accordingly to the Secretary of State to express my reservations about her Department’s proposals. ​ Please click PDF link for Adobe version EARLY DAY MOTIONS ​ ​ Please click PDF to read HUNTING ACT I fully appreciate the desire on the part of some to further amend and strengthen the Hunting Act (2004) which was, to say the least, an imperfect piece of legislation. I yield to nobody in my commitment to seek an end to the hunting of wild animals with dogs: in the early days I was one of very few Conservative Members of Parliament (in the blessed company of the late Sir David Amess and the late Sir Teddy Taylor) who were prepared to voice antihunt opinions. The legislation introduced under Blair's Government, which in the view of many did not go f

Westminster Speakers Panel of Chairmen. Roger's involvement The Speakers Panel of Chairmen is appointed by Mr. Speaker to chair Legislative Standing Committees, Delegated legislation and statutory instruments, Debates in Westminster Hall and Bills taken as a Committee of the Whole House (e.g. sections of the Finance Bill) on the floor of the Main Chamber. In the Chair, members act for Mr. Speaker to manage the business, do not participate in debates and remain non-partisan. Traditionally, Chairmen do not vote on the second reading of bills that they are due to chair and neither do they vote on any of the further stages of the bill. (Hence apparent deficiencies in a busy Chairman’s voting record!) Sessions normally commence and end with delegated (secondary) legislation which takes one session only in committee. I would expect to take the lead chairmanship of two major Government Bills,(such as the Gambling Bill or the Finance Bill) , perhaps a further two minor Bills (such as the Drugs Bill) and a further one or two Private Members` Bills in the course of a session in addition to European Standing Committee, Grand Committee and Westminster Hall Chairmanships. ​ Legislation: Since his appointment to the panel in 1997 Roger Gale has handled the following legislation: Session 2017/2018 European Union (Notification of Withdrawal) Bill Session 2015/2016 The Finance Bill Draft Patents Order Draft Climate Contact Roger ​ ​ ​ ​ ​ *IMPORTANT* Please sign the consent form on this page to allow Sir Roger Gale M.P. to make representations on your behalf and receive any and such information as may be relevant to your concerns. If you need to contact Roger please complete the contact form on this page. However, if you need to contact Roger's office in connection with a communication that you have already started, then please either telephone the office in Birchington on 01843 848588 or e-Mail to either galerj@parliament.uk or suzy@galemail.com ​ When contacting Roge r or Suzy, please always put your full name, your address and also your contact telephone number(s). ​ Send us a message and we’ll get back to you shortly. First name * Last name * Title * Email * Full address * Post Code * Phone * Write a message NINO or other relevant reference numbers. Have you ever been in contact with Roger before? * Have your contact details changed? * Send YOUR M.P. At Your Service SIR ROGER GALE Post-Pandemic your MP, Sir Roger Gale (Herne Bay and Sandwich, including West Thanet) continues to hold face to face surgery appointments at our office in Birchington, where these are necessary and appropriate. ​ All Saturday surgeries are now held at the Birchington office - most Saturday mornings at 215a Canterbury Road, Birchington, CT7 9AH. Please ring 01843 848588 (mornings from 10 a.m.) for an appointment etc. ​ Roger is also very willing to conduct telephone consultations (which very often expedite matters rather than having to wait for a formal “advice surgery”). ​ Should you wish to speak to Roger personally then please email Suzy on suzy@galemail.com - she will be pleased to organise a telephone or ZOOM appointment for you. Alternatively, click on the form below and send in full details. ​ Ring: 01843 848588 (a.m. from 10am) Web: www.rogergale.com Email: galerj@parliament.uk Write: House of Commons Westminster London SW1A 0AA Anchor 1 The GDPR new law comes into effect in May 2018 which affects the way we keep information. We send out emails to my constituents but my office will never pass on your details to a third party unless you have specifically asked for my assistance in a particular matter. Should you wish to be removed from our mailing lists please do let us know your details by post or email - together with your full name and postal address - and we will confirm that your details have been removed. ​ *IMPORTANT* Please sign the consent form to allow Sir Roger Gale M.P. to make representations on your behalf and receive any and such information as may be relevant to your concerns. ​ Connect with Roger 01843 848588 GALERJ@PARLIAMENT.UK Read more on GDPR Law Useful Links © 2025 SLM bottom of page
